name: fused MoE gating: softmax + top-k + optional renormalize

op.signature: (topk_weights[M,topk] fp32, topk_ids[M,topk] int32) = topk(softmax(gating_output[M,E] / fp32)); optional renorm

preconditions: 1 <= topk <= E (topk is a dispatch kwarg, not a tensor shape, so it cannot bind in the decidable constraint mini-language at retrieval time; the reference asserts it at runtime)

topk_weights and topk_ids are returned in DESCENDING probability order within each row, with ties broken by ASCENDING expert id (a stable descending argsort). This is a CONTRACT requirement, not an implementation detail: bf16/fp16 logits quantize many experts to the same fp32 probability, so exact ties are NOT measure-zero, and every backend must agree element-wise. The Triton device kernel achieves this for free because tl.argmax returns the LOWEST index on ties.
softmax is computed over ALL E experts first, then top-k, then optional renorm — NOT softmax over the selected experts only (sgl_kernel.topk_softmax / DeepSeek-V3 convention)

numerics.notes: Pure-torch oracle: fp32 softmax (logits upcast) -> stable descending argsort (ties by ascending expert id) -> top-k -> optional fp32 renorm. NOT torch.topk (its tie-break is unspecified and diverges from tl.argmax, false-failing element-wise parity on bf16 inputs where quantization makes exact ties common). The top-k SELECTION is integer-exact (an id mismatch is abs_err >= 1 and fails any tolerance); the weight VALUES carry the softmax rounding (tl.exp vs torch.exp). The by_dtype tolerance covers a future Triton device kernel's fp32 reduction order. NOTE the top-k discontinuity remains for ADVERSARIAL inputs with near-equal (but not bit-identical) probabilities: a 1-ULP rounding flip can swap the selected id; compare ids by set-equality for such cases. Cross-backend rtol is the fp32-softmax bar, intentionally >= single-backend rtol.
